content: Which of the following statement is correct for oleum?

options.content: It is prepared by adsorption of $$\mathrm{SO}_3$$ in conc. $$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$$

explanation:
Oleum, i.e. $$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{S}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$$ can be prepared by adsorption of $$\mathrm{SO}_3$$ in conc. $$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$$ as follows :

$$\mathrm{SO}_3+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \longrightarrow \underset{\text { Oleum }}{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{S}_2 \mathrm{O}_7}$$

It's structure is as follows :

Thus, it has no $$\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{O}$$ bond and has $$2-\mathrm{OH}$$ groups. Thus, option (a) is the correct.

content: Which of the following inert gas participate in chemical reaction?
explanation:
Neil Bartlett prepared a red compound, $$\mathrm{O}_2^{+}[\mathrm{PtF}_6]^{-}$$ by reacting platinum hexafluoride $$(\mathrm{PtF}_6)$$ with oxygen.

He found that ionisation energy of $$\mathrm{Xe}(1170 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1})$$ is nearly equal to that of molecular oxygen $$(1175 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1})$$ available. Therefore, he was able to form compound of xenon.

$$\mathrm{Xe}+\mathrm{PtF}_6 \longrightarrow \mathrm{Xe}^{+}\left[\mathrm{PtF}_6\right]$$, On the other hand $$\mathrm{He}$$ and $$\mathrm{Ne}$$ has very high value of ionisation energy.

Therefore, only Xe can participate in chemical reactions and form compounds.

Hence, option (a) is the correct.

content: Affinity for hydrogen decreases in the group from fluorine to iodine. Which of the halogen acids should have highest bond dissociation enthalpy?
explanation:
As the size of the halogen atom increases from $$\mathrm{F}$$ to $$\mathrm{I}, \mathrm{H}-X$$ bond length in halogen acids also increases from $$\mathrm{H}-\mathrm{F}$$ to $$\mathrm{HI}$$.

$$(\mathrm{HF}<\mathrm{HCl}<\mathrm{HBr}<\mathrm{HI})$$. The increase in $$\mathrm{H} X$$ bond length decreases the strength of $$\mathrm{H} X$$ bond from $$\mathrm{HF}$$ to $$\mathrm{HI}(\mathrm{HF}>\mathrm{HCl}>\mathrm{HBr}>\mathrm{HI})$$.

Due to successive decrease in the strength of $$\mathrm{H} X$$ bonds, bond dissociation enthalpy decreases from $$\mathrm{HF}$$ to $$\mathrm{HI}$$.

content: Which of the following oxyacid does not contain $$\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{P}$$ bond?
explanation: Hypophosphoric acid $$\left(\mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_6\right)$$ does not contain $$\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{P}$$ bond whereas, isohypophosphoric acid $$\left(\mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_6\right)$$, diphosphorus acid $$\left(\mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_5\right)$$ and diphosphoric acid $$\left(\mathrm{H}_4 \mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_7\right)$$ all contain $$\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{P}$$ bonds. The structures of the following oxyacids of phosphorus is shown below:

$$\mathop {8N{H_3}}\limits_{(Excess)} + 3C{l_2}\buildrel {} \over \longrightarrow X$$

$$N{H_3} + \mathop {3C{l_2}}\limits_{(Excess)} \buildrel {} \over \longrightarrow Y$$

What is X and Y in the above reaction?
Which of the following products will be obtained when copper metal is reacted with $$\mathrm{HNO}_3$$ ?
explanation:
The products of the reaction of copper with $$\mathrm{HNO}_3$$ depends upon the concentration of $$\mathrm{HNO}_3$$ used.

Copper metal reacts with dilute $$\mathrm{HNO}_3$$ to form nitrogen (II) oxide (NO).

$$3 \mathrm{Cu}+8 \mathrm{HNO}_3 \text { (dil.) } \longrightarrow 3 \mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2+2 \mathrm{NO}+4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$$

Copper metal reacts with conc. $$\mathrm{HNO}_3$$ to form nitrogen (IV) oxide or nitrogen dioxide $$(\mathrm{NO}_2)$$

$$\mathrm{Cu}+4 \mathrm{HNO}_3 \text { (conc.) } \longrightarrow \mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2+2 \mathrm{NO}_2+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$$

content: A green yellow gas reacts with an alkali metal hydroxide to form a halate which can be used in fireworks and safety matches. The gas and halate are, respectively
explanation:
A halate will be formed from halogen and the greenish yellow gas is $$\mathrm{Cl}_2$$.

The halate which is used in fireworks and safety matches is $$\mathrm{KClO}_3$$. The reaction is as follow

$$3 \mathrm{Cl}_2+6 \mathrm{KOH} \longrightarrow \mathrm{KClO}_3+5 \mathrm{HCl}+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$$

content:
Assertion (A) $$\mathrm{N}_2$$ is less reactive than $$\mathrm{P}_4$$.

Reason (R) Nitrogen has more electron gain enthalpy than phosphorus.

options.content: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).

explanation: Assertion is true but reason is false. $$\mathrm{N}_2$$ is less reactive than $$\mathrm{P}_4$$ due to high value of bond dissociation energy, which is because of the presence of triple bond between two $$\mathrm{N}$$-atoms of $$\mathrm{N}_2$$ molecule.

content:
Assertion (A) $$\mathrm{NaCl}$$ reacts with concentrated $$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$$ to give colourless fumes with pungent smell. But on adding $$\mathrm{MnO}_2$$ the fumes become greenish yellow.

Reason (R) $$\mathrm{MnO}_2$$ oxidises $$\mathrm{HCl}$$ to chlorine gas which is greenish yellow.

options.content: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).

explanation:
Both assertion and reason is correct and reason is the correct explanation of the assertion. $$\mathrm{NaCl}$$ reacts with concentrated $$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$$ to give colourless fumes with pungent smell. This pungent smell is due to formation of $$\mathrm{HCl}$$.

$$\mathrm{NaCl}+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \longrightarrow \mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4+2 \mathrm{HCl}$$

But on adding $$\mathrm{MnO}_2$$, the fumes become greenish yellow due to formation of chlorine gas.
